response.justification: The candidate clears the minimum bar of 15+ years in software engineering and a technical bachelor's degree, with genuinely broad hands-on experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput low-latency systems, plus entrepreneurial open-source work. However, the role is a director-level IC position centered on AI strategy, recognized AI expertise, and advising strategic customers, and the resume shows only about a year of recent AI-agent tooling work with no demonstrated AI products, publications, or thought leadership. The career trajectory is senior/staff-level delivery engineering rather than principal-level strategic influence, making the fit weak despite solid engineering fundamentals.
strengths: 19+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-scale low-latency systems
Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work at Naver (MCP code generators, agentic workflow tooling)
Entrepreneurial open-source founder (GISCollective) with a track record of conference talks and teaching
concerns: No recognized AI expertise, AI-built products at scale, or advanced degree in an AI-related field
Trajectory is senior/staff IC delivery work, not director-level strategy, customer advisory, or organizational influence
Little evidence of cloud platform strategy experience; AI exposure is limited to roughly the past year

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise and AI product track record
Seniority gap versus director-level strategic IC role
Meets 15+ years experience with broad, versatile engineering background
